General [Tasker] Preferred Network Mode Profile (Switching to 3G/5G)

  • Thread starter austinjklim
  • Start date Aug 13, 2025
  • Tags
    network preferred network mode switch tasker
  • Home
  • Forums
  • Google
  • Google Pixel 8 Pro

Breadcrumb

  • Home
  • Forums
  • Google
  • Google Pixel 8 Pro
    • Search This thread
    By:
    Advanced…
    A

    austinjklim

    Senior Member
    Mar 25, 2012
    178
    35
    Kota Kinabalu
    • Aug 13, 2025
    • #1
    In short, just got the phone to be used as my secondary device, the only problem was the idle battery drain was terrible then my primary Poco F6. Weeks of troubleshooting later, GMS and modem wakelocks were the biggest culprit. Fixed the GMS wakelock with enhancedoze and tasker, modem on the other hand was a tricky one.

    In the end, switching to 3G instead did reduced the idle battery drain, but tasker did not allow due to restricted API levels. So the only solution that I had working for me was to use AutoInput with Tasker, to switch 3G/5G with a task.

    Thus, this profile was born. Create a task shortcut widget on your homescreen to turn on 3G / 5G for easy switching. You can use it as a base and customize yourself according to your needs. Hope it helps anyone out there. Technically this should work for all devices, but your kilometer may vary. Cheers.
     

    Attachments

    • MINE.prj.xml
      24.8 KB · Views: 43

    rodken

    Recognized Contributor

    austinjklim said:
    In short, just got the phone to be used as my secondary device, the only problem was the idle battery drain was terrible then my primary Poco F6. Weeks of troubleshooting later, GMS and modem wakelocks were the biggest culprit. Fixed the GMS wakelock with enhancedoze and tasker, modem on the other hand was a tricky one.

    In the end, switching to 3G instead did reduced the idle battery drain, but tasker did not allow due to restricted API levels. So the only solution that I had working for me was to use AutoInput with Tasker, to switch 3G/5G with a task.

    Thus, this profile was born. Create a task shortcut widget on your homescreen to turn on 3G / 5G for easy switching. You can use it as a base and customize yourself according to your needs. Hope it helps anyone out there. Technically this should work for all devices, but your kilometer may vary. Cheers.
    Click to expand...
    Click to collapse
    If your device is rooted, you can bypass the API restrictions entirely. The reason Tasker failed is that Android versions from API level 21 and higher have deprecated direct programmatic control of network modes for non-system apps.

    The settings command can be used via ADB or a terminal emulator on the device
    Code: 
    # To get the current network mode setting
adb shell settings get global preferred_network_mode
# To change to LTE/WCDMA (3G/4G)
adb shell settings put global preferred_network_mode 9
# To change back to NR/LTE/WCDMA (3G/4G/5G)
adb shell settings put global preferred_network_mode 22
     
    Reactions: cmh714 and Dr.No.pt
    A

    The settings command can be used via ADB or a terminal emulator on the device
    Code:

# To change to LTE/WCDMA (3G/4G)

# To change back to NR/LTE/WCDMA (3G/4G/5G)

    Click to expand...
    Click to collapse
    I did use adb shell but it would not work, same goes for run shell on tasker for preferred network mode. Maybe it works for you on other ROMs. I am on CRdroid.
     
    Reactions: rodken

    rodken

    Recognized Contributor
    Jan 11, 2010

    • Aug 14, 2025
    • #4
    austinjklim said:
    I did use adb shell but it would not work, same goes for run shell on tasker for preferred network mode. Maybe it works for you on other ROMs. I am on CRdroid.
    Click to expand...
    Click to collapse
    Gotcha. So, the issue might be specific to crDroid Different ROMs are built on different codebases and have levels of stability and power management optimizations. A crDroid might have a better modem implementation.

    I always thought that on some customs, you need to grant the app DUMP permission which you can do this via ADB.
    Code: 
    adb -d shell pm grant com.asksven.betterbatterystats android.permission.DUMP
    This gives the app the ability to access and read system logs to battery stats, which are usually restricted, but that's for another day or another thread. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
